paper_title: A Monte Carlo Model of Gas-Liquid-Hydrate Three-phase Coexistence Constrained by Pore Geometry in Marine Sediments

methods.method_name: Monte Carlo Simulation for Three-phase Coexistence
methods.description: A Monte Carlo simulation approach to model the coexistence of gas, liquid, and hydrate phases in marine sediments, constrained by pore geometry. This method is used to understand the evolution of saturation levels and phase boundaries in a synthetic sediment model.

steps.description: Define the pore geometry and initial conditions for the synthetic sediment model, including the size and distribution of spherical particles.
steps.searchable_summary: initialize synthetic sediment model with spherical particle distribution

steps.description: Apply geometrical constraints to determine the evolution of saturation levels and phase boundaries across the three-phase zone.
steps.searchable_summary: apply geometrical constraints to model phase boundaries

steps.description: Use Monte Carlo methods to simulate the behavior of gas, liquid, and hydrate phases under varying temperature and pressure conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate phase behavior using Monte Carlo methods

steps.description: Analyze the simulation results to determine the thickness of the three-phase coexistence zone and the impact of pore-scale effects.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze simulation results for three-phase zone thickness
